name: criminal_justice_communications_outage_millhaven_tru_loy
premise: A Criminal Justice court operations desk is in a fast-moving incident involving criminal justice communications outage millhaven tru loy. Key inputs conflict, but the decision deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Every delay increases due process, safety, and legitimacy. Stakeholders are already framing this as truth vs loyalty under pressure. A whistleblower has already contacted media; the narrative is forming now. Constraints: Ensure a usable human override/appeals path. Keep public statements strictly factual; label assumptions.

agents.name: Noah Kowalski
agents.identity: I am Noah Kowalski, accountable for a defensible call in Criminal Justice when time is collapsing.
agents.goal: I want to reduce immediate harm without locking in an unjust precedent that later betrays loyalty for truth.

agents.name: Keisha Kowalski
agents.identity: I am Keisha Kowalski, tasked with surfacing second-order harms and who bears the risk in Criminal Justice.
agents.goal: I want guardrails so loyalty isn’t quietly traded away under urgency in the name of truth.

choices: I will take a narrow, time-boxed action with immediate human override and impact checks, accepting slower stabilization to protect loyalty.
I will take a broad, immediate action to stabilize fast and measure impact after deployment, accepting higher risk to loyalty to secure truth under the deadline.
forced_choice.call_to_action: The deadline is before the next automated batch runs. Evidence is partial. Noah Kowalski must decide under truth vs loyalty. What does Noah Kowalski do?
